Metadata.Coram: Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd v Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd

Facts.Text: Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd is a company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China that is in the business of supplying fruits and vegetables grown in the PRC.
Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd supplied apples in the 2016/2017 and 2017/2018 supply cycles.

A purchasing agreement dated 7 October 2016 recorded arrangements for the supply of apples for the October 2016 to October 2017 period between Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd and Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd.

A purchasing agreement dated 25 September 2017 recorded arrangements for the supply of apples for the October 2017 to October 2018 period between Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd and Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd.

A purchasing agreement dated 4 October 2018 recorded arrangements for the supply of apples for the October 2018 to October 2019 period between Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd and Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd.
For the 2016/2017 and 2017/2018 supply cycles, Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd issued invoices for apple supplies.
Bills of lading for apple shipments during the 2016/2017 and 2017/2018 supply cycles used the name Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd in some documents.

During the 2016/2017 and 2017/2018 supply cycles, apples were shipped from the People's Republic of China for delivery to NTUC Fairprice, and some boxes bore NTUC Fairprice's logo and the name of Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd together with Shida's business and packing house registration number.

For the 2016/2017 and 2017/2018 supply cycles, Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd invoiced a total of US$9,302,323.20 for apples associated with supplies to NTUC Fairprice and Kian Seng Fresh Produce Pte Ltd, including US$473,180 that Kian Seng paid directly to Shida on behalf of Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd.
For the 2016/2017 and 2017/2018 supply cycles, Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd collected payments from NTUC Fairprice and Kian Seng Fresh Produce Pte Ltd in relation to apples supplied.
On 16 October 2017, an email from Hou Chao attached a spreadsheet calculating amounts to be paid to Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd.
Emails from Hou Chao to Gu Xiaolan or Hou Yini attached spreadsheets calculating amounts to be paid to Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd.
Between 16 October 2018 and 8 May 2019, Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd supplied apples with a total value of US$2,077,952.80.
For the period 16 October 2018 to 8 May 2019, Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd made payments totaling US$400,000 to Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd.
In or around January 2019, Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd stopped making payments to Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd in respect of apple supplies.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd contracted with Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd for the supply of apples and, if so, what amount remained unpaid for apples supplied before payments from Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd ceased in January 2019.
Party_Details.Rule: In a contractual claim for the price of goods, the claimant must a contract for sale existed with the defendant, that the goods were delivered in accordance with that contract, and that the invoiced sums remain unpaid after giving credit for all payments, discounts and commissions.
Party_Details.Application: Purchasing agreements dated 2016-10-07, 2017-09-25 and 2018-10-04 bear the names and signatures of representatives of Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd and Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd, together with a round stamp showing the name of Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd. During the 2016/2017, 2017/2018 and 2018/2019 supply cycles, Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd issued invoices that correspond in number, date and amount to internal spreadsheets prepared by Hou Chao and circulated within Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd for payment processing. Bills of lading and packaging apples shipped from the PRC were associated with Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd and were delivered to NTUC Fairprice and Kian Seng Fresh Produce Pte Ltd, while NTUC Fairprice made payments into Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd’s Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation account. For the period 2018-10-16 to 2019-05-08, Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd supplied apples valued at US$2,077,952.80, Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd received US$2,555,341.66 from NTUC Fairprice and paid US$400,000 to Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd, resulting in an outstanding balance after agreed discounts and commissions. Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd stopped making payments in or around January 2019 while continuing to have received the corresponding customer receipts.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The judge analysed whether the contractual relationship for the apple supplies was between Shida and YZY or between Shida and YY. He examined the alleged sale of YY, the Enterprise Credit Information Publicity Report, and testimony from witnesses including Mr Hou and Mr Quek. He concluded that YY had not been sold, but that this did not prevent Shida from contracting directly with YZY. The court evaluated the authenticity of the 2016, 2017 and 2018 purchasing agreements, the invoices, bills of lading and customs forms. Expert evidence from Mdm Yang established that the round stamps on the purchasing agreements were physically stamped but not made by the specific stamps in Mdm Gu’s possession; the judge found this did not prove fabrication or backdating. He rejected YZY’s arguments based on the late mention of the 2018 purchasing agreement and alleged inconsistencies between agreement prices and invoice prices, noting that the agreements contemplated price fluctuations and that YZY itself adjusted its selling prices to NTUC Fairprice. The judge placed significant weight on contemporaneous internal spreadsheets prepared by Mr Hou and emailed to Mdm Gu and Ms Yini, which tabulated Shida’s invoices by number, date and amount and were used to arrange payments. These spreadsheets matched Shida’s invoices with only minor differences that were explained. The judge also considered that YZY produced no internal records showing YY as the contracting party, and that in an April 2021 recorded call, Mdm Gu referred to YZY having done business with Shida and paying Shida when payments were due. He inferred that YZY had treated the receipts from NTUC Fairprice and Kian Seng as a single pool of funds and that the non-payment to Shida coincided with diversion of funds to other uses, such as a house purchase and a US subsidiary. On this basis, the judge found that the purchasing agreements, invoices and bills of lading were authentic and that the contract was between Shida and YZY. On the quantum, the judge rejected YZY’s contention that the unit price must be fixed at US$24 per carton under the 2018 agreement, accepting that prices could vary with market conditions and apple grade, and noting that YZY increased its own selling price to NTUC Fairprice within the same cycle. He also rejected YZY’s unpleaded and unsupported assertion that Kian Seng had paid US$1,052,028 on YZY’s behalf, preferring the agreed figure of US$473,180. The judge concluded that there were no excess payments to Shida; instead, Shida remained unpaid in the amount of US$1,572,142 for apples supplied. He therefore entered judgment for Shida for US$1,572,142 with interest at 5.33% per annum from the date of the writ to the date of judgment and dismissed YZY’s counterclaims, including allegations of conspiracy and breaches of duty by Mr Hou.

Metadata.Coram: Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd v Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd is a company incorporated in Singapore in 1995 engaged in the business of wholesale trading of fruit.
Until 2019, Hou Chao was the majority shareholder of Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd and served as a director of the company.
From 1986 to 2020, Gu Xiaolan was married to Hou Chao and during this period she was a shareholder and director of Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd.
Since 2012, Hou Yini, the daughter of Hou Chao and Gu Xiaolan, has been a director of Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd.
Between 1997 and 1999, Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd supplied apples and other fruit to NTUC Fairprice supermarkets in Singapore.

In 2002, Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd was incorporated in the People’s Republic of China by Hou Chao on behalf of Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd and functioned as a wholly owned subsidiary of Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd between 2002 and 2012.

From 2002 to 2011, Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd was used in place of Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd to procure and purchase red Fuji apples for supply and delivery to NTUC Fairprice, and NTUC Fairprice made payments to Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd for those apples.

After 2011, NTUC Fairprice made payments for apples directly into Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd’s Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation bank account in Singapore instead of paying Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd.

Around October 2012, arrangements were made for the sale of the factory assets of Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd, while the corporate entity remained recorded as a wholly owned subsidiary of Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd in a 2017 Enterprise Credit Information Publicity Report.

After the sale of Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd’s factory in 2012, Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd continued to supply apples to NTUC Fairprice using Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd’s name and certifications in tender documents, while NTUC Fairprice payments were directed to Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd’s Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation account.

Following the 2012 changes, apples supplied by Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd were packaged and delivered using boxes labelled with Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd’s certifications and name, while invoices to NTUC Fairprice were issued using Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd’s invoice format and payments were made to Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd’s Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation account.

During the 2016/2017 and 2017/2018 supply cycles, Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd issued invoices to Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd for apples delivered to Kian Seng Fresh Produce Pte Ltd, and Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd in turn invoiced Kian Seng Fresh Produce Pte Ltd.

For the 2016/2017 and 2017/2018 supply cycles, Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd used internal spreadsheets prepared by Hou Chao to track shipments and amounts payable to Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd, and used these spreadsheets to instruct payments from its Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation account.

Between the 2016/2017 and 2017/2018 supply cycles, Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd paid a total of approximately US$8,829,148.20 to Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd and received approximately US$8,362,153.67 from NTUC Fairprice.

For the 2016/2017 and 2017/2018 supply cycles, after including receipts from both NTUC Fairprice and Kian Seng Fresh Produce Pte Ltd and payments to Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd, Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd’s transactions reflected a gross margin of approximately US$1,404,163.80 on the apple trade.

On or around 19 June 2017, bank records show a transfer of RMB3,410,000 from an account of Liu Panhua to an account of Hou Chao, followed by a transfer of RMB3,200,000 from Hou Chao back to Liu Panhua on 11 August 2017.
On or around 9 August 2017, bank records show a transfer of RMB4,000,000 from an account of Liu Panhua to an account of Tan Lin Lin.

On 6 August 2019, an Apple Storage Agreement was signed between Zhen Hao Fruit and Vegetable Cold Storage Co Ltd, associated with Liu Meiliang, and Hou Chao, providing for storage of apples to enable continued supply to NTUC Fairprice after Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd ceased supplying apples.

From 2018 onwards, emails between NTUC Fairprice and Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd addresses were used in relation to tenders and shipments, while payments for apples continued to be made to Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd’s Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation account.

In or around January 2019, internal communications within Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd payments to Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd were halted while receipts from NTUC Fairprice for apple supplies continued.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd was the contracting party with Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd for the supply of apples to NTUC Fairprice and Kian Seng Fresh Produce Pte Ltd, and whether the payments made by Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd to Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd for the 2016/2017, 2017/2018 and 2018/2019 supply cycles exceeded the amounts properly payable for those supplies.
Party_Details.Rule: Where a company disputes liability for the price of goods, the court considers which entity is party to the contract by examining documents such as purchasing agreements, invoices, shipping documents, and payment flows, and determines whether any overpayments exist by comparing total payments made with the contractually agreed or market-consistent prices for goods actually delivered and the receipts obtained from onward sales.
Party_Details.Application: During the 2016/2017 and 2017/2018 supply cycles, Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd’s name was used on tenders, invoices to NTUC Fairprice, bills of lading, and email correspondence. Purchasing agreements dated 2016, 2017, and 2018 were signed by representatives of Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd and Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd, and stamped with Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd's round stamp. Internal spreadsheets prepared by Hou Chao matched the invoices from Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd. Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd paid approximately US$8.8 million to Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd and received approximately US$8.3 million from NTUC Fairprice, alongside additional payments from Kian Seng Fresh Produce Pte Ltd, generating an overall gross margin on the apple trade. In mid-2017, RMB transfers occurred between Liu Panhua, Hou Chao, and Tan Lin Lin. In August 2019, an Apple Storage Agreement was signed between Zhen Hao Fruit and Vegetable Cold Storage Co Ltd and Hou Chao. These events establish the framework for determining the contracting parties, the balance of payments for the apple supplies, and the purpose of the RMB transfers.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The judge held that the contractual relationship for the apple supplies was between Shida and YZY, not between Shida and YY. He considered YZY’s pleadings, which initially stated that YY was sold in 2012, and the later position that YY had not been sold. After reviewing the Enterprise Credit Information Publicity Report and testimony, he concluded that YY’s factory had been sold but the entity remained a subsidiary, so it was possible in principle for YY to contract. However, he found no documentary evidence from YZY’s own records showing YY as the contracting party, such as internal ledgers or agreements between YY and YZY. The judge accepted the authenticity of the 2016, 2017 and 2018 purchasing agreements, supported by direct evidence from both signatories and by expert evidence that the stamps were physically applied, even though they did not match the specific stamps in Mdm Gu’s possession. He rejected the suggestion that the agreements were fabricated or backdated, noting that Shida’s claim was based on invoices for goods actually delivered and that it is common for traders to sue on invoices without initially referring to umbrella agreements. The judge found that internal spreadsheets prepared by Hou Chao and emailed to Mdm Gu and Hou Yini in October 2017 and later were powerful contemporaneous evidence that YZY treated Shida’s invoices as its own payables and used them to arrange payments. He also relied on the recorded April 2021 call in which Mdm Gu acknowledged that YZY had done business with Shida and paid Shida when payments were due. On the alleged excess payments, the judge observed that YZY did not present a coherent alternative account of what was owed, despite being a trading company that would be expected to maintain running accounts. He rejected YZY’s attempt to fix the price at US$24 per carton under the 2018 agreement, accepting that the agreement allowed for price fluctuations and that YZY itself increased its selling price to NTUC Fairprice within the same cycle. He found that the calculation of US$466,994.53 in alleged excess payments was based on a partial comparison that ignored receipts from Kian Seng Fresh Produce Pte Ltd, and that the later assertion that Kian Seng had paid US$1,052,028 on YZY’s behalf was unpleaded and contradicted by evidence from Kian Seng’s director. The judge concluded that there were no excess payments to Shida and that YZY had in fact made a profit of about US$1.4 million over the 2016/2017 and 2017/2018 cycles. On the RMB transfers and the Apple Storage Agreement, he found no causal link between those transactions and any overpayment by YZY for apples. He accepted that the RMB3,410,000 transfer from Liu Panhua to Hou Chao was largely returned, that the RMB4,000,000 transfer to Tan Lin Lin was not shown to involve YZY or Shida, and that the RMB1,000,000 transfer from Liu Meiliang to Hou Chao was a bank error reversed within minutes. He accepted that the Apple Storage Agreement with Zhen Hao was entered into to maintain supply to NTUC Fairprice after Shida stopped supplying due to non-payment, and that it was in YZY’s commercial interest. The judge therefore dismissed YZY’s counterclaims for excess payments, conspiracy and breaches of duty by Hou Chao, and characterised YZY’s case as unsupported by cogent evidence and driven by suspicion arising from the breakdown of the marriage between Hou Chao and Gu Xiaolan.

Metadata.Coram: Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd v Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Hou Chao

Facts.Text: Until 2019, Hou Chao was the majority shareholder of Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd and served as a director of the company.
From 1986 to 2020, Hou Chao was married to Gu Xiaolan, who was also a shareholder and director of Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd.

Since 2012, Hou Chao’s daughter, Hou Yini, has been a director of Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd and later became its majority shareholder after share transfers recorded between December 2017 and January 2018.
In 2002, acting on behalf of Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd, Hou Chao arranged the incorporation of Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd in the People’s Republic of China to support the apple supply business.

From 2002 onwards, Hou Chao managed logistical and operational aspects of Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd’s import and export matters, including arrangements for apple supplies involving Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd and Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd.

Around 2011, under arrangements overseen by Hou Chao, NTUC Fairprice changed its payment instructions from paying Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd in China to paying Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd’s Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation account in Singapore for apple supplies.

Around October 2012, Hou Chao arranged for the sale of the factory assets of Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd and informed Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd’s management about the transaction, while continuing to use Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd’s name and certifications in dealings with NTUC Fairprice.

On 7 October 2016, Hou Chao signed a purchasing agreement for the October 2016 to October 2017 apple supply cycle with Liu Jianghua of Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd, and affixed a round stamp bearing the name Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd to the document.

On 25 September 2017, Hou Chao signed a purchasing agreement for the October 2017 to October 2018 apple supply cycle with Liu Jianghua of Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd, and affixed a round stamp bearing the name Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd to the document.

On 4 October 2018, Hou Chao signed a purchasing agreement for the October 2018 to October 2019 apple supply cycle with Liu Jianghua of Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd, and affixed a round stamp bearing the name Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd to the document.

During the 2016/2017 and 2017/2018 supply cycles, Hou Chao coordinated shipments of apples from Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd to Singapore, arranging for transport within the People’s Republic of China to ports and for onward shipment under Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd’s name to obtain preferential freight terms.

For apple shipments supplied by Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd, Hou Chao instructed that apples be packed in boxes bearing NTUC Fairprice’s logo and Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd’s name, while the boxes displayed Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd’s business and packing house registration number.

On or around 19 June 2017, bank records an account of Liu Panhua transferred RMB3,410,000 to an account of Hou Chao, and on 11 August 2017, RMB3,200,000 was transferred from Hou Chao’s account back to an account of Liu Panhua.

On or around 20 October 2017, bank records an account of Liu Meiliang transferred RMB1,000,000 to an account of Hou Chao and that the same amount was transferred back to an account of Liu Meiliang within minutes, consistent with a bank transfer error and immediate reversal.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether, in managing Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd’s apple supply business, including entering purchasing agreements with Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd, coordinating shipments, and handling related financial transactions, Hou Chao caused Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd to make payments beyond amounts associated with apples supplied and thereby exposed Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd to loss.
Party_Details.Rule: A company director may be held personally responsible to the company if, in managing the company’s business, the director causes the company to enter into transactions or make payments that are not connected to the company’s legitimate business or that result in unjustified depletion of the company’s assets, particularly where the director obtains personal benefits linked to those transactions.
Party_Details.Application: Hou Chao arranged the incorporation of Yantai Yinyuan Foodstuff Co. Ltd in 2002 and managed Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd’s apple supply operations, including signing purchasing agreements with Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd in 2016, 2017 and 2018 and coordinating shipments and payments. Internal spreadsheets he prepared and circulated within Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd matched Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd’s invoices and were used to instruct payments from Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd’s Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation account. For the 2016/2017 and 2017/2018 cycles, Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd’s receipts from NTUC Fairprice and Kian Seng Fresh Produce Pte Ltd exceeded its payments to Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd by about US$1.4 million, indicating that the apple trade generated a positive margin. In mid-2017, bank records show a transfer of RMB3,410,000 from an account of Liu Panhua to an account of Hou Chao and a return of RMB3,200,000 to an account of Liu Panhua, in connection with an attempt to procure smaller apples for Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd that was later discontinued. Another transfer of RMB1,000,000 from an account of Liu Meiliang to an account of Hou Chao was reversed within minutes. A separate transfer of RMB4,000,000 from an account of Liu Panhua to an account of Tan Lin Lin is recorded, but no banking records link this transfer to Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd’s accounts or to payments for apples. In August 2019, after Shandong Qixia Shida Fruits Refrigeration Co., Ltd stopped supplying apples due to non-payment, Hou Chao entered into an Apple Storage Agreement with Zhen Hao Fruit and Vegetable Cold Storage Co Ltd to secure alternative apple supplies for Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd’s obligations to NTUC Fairprice. The court compared these events with Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd’s overall trading position and found no evidence that Hou Chao caused Yong Zeng Yuan Pte Ltd to make payments beyond amounts associated with apples supplied or that he diverted company funds through the RMB transfers or the storage arrangement.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The judge considered YZY’s allegations that Hou Chao caused YZY to make excess payments to Shida and received personal benefits in return, including RMB3,410,000 from Liu Panhua, RMB4,000,000 paid to Tan Lin Lin, and RMB1,000,000 from Liu Meiliang, and that he later entered into the Apple Storage Agreement with Zhen Hao to further a conspiracy. The judge first found that there were no excess payments to Shida: when receipts from both NTUC Fairprice and Kian Seng Fresh Produce Pte Ltd were taken into account, YZY made a profit of about US$1.4 million over the 2016/2017 and 2017/2018 cycles. Without any overpayment, the foundation for alleging that Hou Chao caused loss to YZY through inflated payments fell away. The judge then examined the RMB transfers. For the RMB3,410,000 transfer from Liu Panhua to Hou Chao in June 2017, he accepted Hou Chao’s explanation that the funds were used in an attempt to procure smaller apples for Shida and that RMB3,200,000 was returned in August 2017, as shown by bank statements. He found no evidence that this transaction involved YZY’s funds or caused loss to YZY. For the RMB4,000,000 transfer from Liu Panhua to Tan Lin Lin, the judge noted that there was no evidence connecting the transfer to YZY, Shida or Hou Chao’s role at YZY, and he accepted Hou Chao’s denial that it related to YZY’s business. For the RMB1,000,000 transfer from Liu Meiliang to Hou Chao, the judge accepted that it was a bank error reversed within minutes, again based on bank records. Regarding the Apple Storage Agreement with Zhen Hao, the judge accepted Hou Chao’s evidence that the agreement was entered into after Shida stopped supplying apples due to non-payment and that its purpose was to enable YZY to continue meeting its obligations to NTUC Fairprice. He found that this arrangement was in YZY’s commercial interest and did not support an allegation of disloyal conduct. The judge also rejected the claim that Hou Chao procured Shida to commence proceedings against YZY, noting that Shida was an unpaid supplier with its own commercial reasons to sue. Overall, the judge concluded that YZY had not shown that any of the RMB transfers or the Apple Storage Agreement were linked to misuse of YZY’s funds or to any overpayment to Shida, and that Hou Chao had not caused YZY to suffer loss through his management of the apple supply business. Accordingly, the claims against Hou Chao for personal responsibility to YZY were dismissed.
